businessOverview:
Salesforce, Inc. (NYSE: CRM) is the world's largest customer-relationship-management (CRM) software company and the pioneer of cloud-delivered enterprise SaaS. Founded in 1999, it began as a sales-force-automation tool that proved business software could be delivered over the internet on a subscription, and has since expanded into a multi-cloud enterprise application suite — its 'Customer 360' / 'Data 360' platform — spanning sales, service, marketing, commerce, analytics, integration and collaboration. It generated record FY2026 revenue of ~$41.5B (year ended Jan 31, 2026) at a 34.1% non-GAAP operating margin, and is ranked #1 in CRM applications by worldwide market share (IDC).

Salesforce serves ~150,000+ customers across every industry and size, from small businesses to the bulk of the Fortune 500. Go-to-market combines a large direct enterprise sales force, a deep partner ecosystem (system integrators, ISVs, consulting partners), and the AppExchange marketplace. The model is land-and-expand: customers typically start in one cloud and add more over time, driving multi-cloud cross-sell. Ramp card-spend data corroborates the dominance — Salesforce captures ~70.7% of all CRM category spend across 50k+ businesses (rank #1).

Current strategy centers on AI 'digital labor.' Agentforce lets enterprises deploy autonomous AI agents for service, sales and other workflows, grounded by Data 360 (formerly Data Cloud), which unifies enterprise data so agents act on trusted, governed context. Management positions Agentforce + Data 360 as the fastest-growing products in company history — combined ARR reached ~$3.4B (+200%+ YoY) by Q1 FY2027, with Agentforce alone at $1.2B (+205% YoY).
productModel:
Salesforce's architecture is a unified, multi-tenant cloud platform on which all applications share one metadata and data foundation. The core 'clouds' include Sales Cloud and Service Cloud (its two largest), Marketing and Commerce Cloud, the Platform (custom development, automation and Slack for collaboration), Data 360 (its customer-data platform), Tableau (analytics, 2019), MuleSoft (integration/APIs, 2018), Informatica (data management, 2025) and Agentforce (the agentic-AI layer). Slack (acquired for ~$27.7B in 2021) serves as the conversational front-end and 'agent operating system' for surfacing Agentforce inside the flow of work.

The revenue model is overwhelmingly recurring subscription & support (~95% of revenue) — per-seat term licenses billed annually in advance — which produces high visibility (reflected in RPO of $67.9B and cRPO of $33.6B, +14% YoY) and >90% subscription gross margins. With Agentforce, Salesforce is layering a consumption (usage-based) motion on top of seats: after initially pricing at ~$2 per conversation, it moved in May 2025 to 'Flex Credits,' where each Agentforce action consumes ~$0.10 of credits, sold in packs with pay-as-you-go and pre-commit options.

This seat-plus-consumption hybrid is the central business-model evolution: as AI agents do more 'work,' Salesforce monetizes actions/outcomes rather than only human seats, expanding addressable spend beyond headcount — but introducing lumpier, less predictable consumption revenue. As of Q1 FY2027 Salesforce also revised its disclosure to two subscription buckets — Agentforce Apps ($6.91B, 65% of subscription revenue, +9% YoY) and Data 360, Headless Platform & Other ($3.68B, 35%, +25% YoY) — retiring the old per-cloud splits. The ecosystem (AppExchange, system integrators, the Trailblazer developer community) extends the platform and is a major source of stickiness, increasingly important for rolling out Agentforce at enterprise scale.

leadership.name: Marc Benioff
leadership.title: Chair of the Board & Chief Executive Officer (Co-Founder)
leadership.background: Co-founded Salesforce in 1999; pioneer of cloud SaaS.

leadership.name: Robin Washington
leadership.title: President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer
leadership.background: Appointed Feb 2025; former Gilead CFO. Also principal accounting officer as of March 2026.

leadership.name: Srinivas (Srini) Tallapragada
leadership.title: President & Chief Engineering and Customer Success Officer

leadership.name: David Schmaier
leadership.title: President & Chief Strategy Officer
leadership.background: Former Chief Product Officer; founder of Vlocity (acquired by Salesforce).
